description: Someone who composes poems is a 'weru' (poet). Phi uses 'melira' (song) for a poem as well as a song because written verse remains a work for the voice. A poet may work on the page or entirely through speech and memory.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips for voiced 'w' and move forward into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then draw the tongue back and round the lips into high-back 'u'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A poet can compose and carry verse without writing it down. Rhythm, memory, and repeated recitation let the work travel by voice.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: someone composing patterned works for voice or page
semantic_domains.community: a recognised practice that brings poems into shared memory and speech
